hypothesis: An equivariant graph neural network trained jointly on Fe K-edge XANES spectra and their corresponding 3D local coordination geometries from the XDIP dataset will achieve significantly lower structure-to-spectrum prediction error than non-equivariant GNN baselines (GCN, GAT, GIN) specifically for iron centers with low-symmetry or mixed-valence coordination environments, because explicit encoding of rotational and translational invariance will better capture the geometric sensitivity of pre-edge and edge features to subtle distortions in the first coordination sphere that are systematically misrepresented by scalar node/edge features alone.
